pool.baseline: i was just looking at the stars and it hit me how much of a joke everything is. like, in a few billion years the sun is just gonna die and every single thing we've ever done or felt or built will be gone. just total silence. makes everything feel so pointless, doesnt it? like why even bother trying to get through the day when the end result is always zero.

pool.baseline: everything just feels like too much. i've spent 5 years on this research and it's all falling apart. the funding is gone, the lab is basically shutting down, and i feel like there's literally nothing left of me outside of this work. i'm just so tired of fighting to keep things from collapsing. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very embers of my soul. How cruel it is, that after all the vows exchanged in the moonlight, I am left here in this hollow silence. Every breath feels like a heavy stone, and the world has become a grey, tasteless void. I feel as though I am already a ghost, haunting the ruins of a life that no longer belongs to me. Perhaps it would be more graceful to simply... cease. To let the shadows take me, rather than endure this wretched, lonely waking.

pool.baseline: honestly i was walking through the crowds in the city center today and it just hit me... i could literally just stop existing right now and the flow of people wouldn't even change. like i'm just a ghost in this place. it feels like if i vanished tomorrow, no one would even realize a space was empty.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the fund, the company, all of it. i lost everything in the crash and the debt is just... it's impossible. i can't even think about going back home. the shame of looking my parents in the eye and telling them i blew their life savings is more than i can handle. i feel like i've already died, i'm just waiting for the rest of me to catch up.
